---
title: "SOM"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Self-Organising Map Analysis

This Quarto section assumes that the EDA/pre-processing code has already created the final model-ready dataset called `airbnb_model_data`, and that the reduced interpretable dataset is called `airbnb_reduced`.

```{r som-libraries, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load Required Libraries:
library(tidyverse)
library(kohonen)
library(cluster)
library(scales)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
```

```{r som-input-check}
# Set Seed for Reproducibility:
set.seed(2026)

# Convert Final Model-Ready Data to Matrix Format for SOM:
som_input <- airbnb_model_data |>
  as.matrix()

# Check Dataset Dimensions:
som_input_overview <- tibble(
  Dataset = "SOM input dataset",
  Observations = nrow(som_input),
  Variables = ncol(som_input)
)

# Output SOM Input Overview Table:
som_input_overview_latex <- som_input_overview |>
  kable(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    caption = "Dataset Used for Self-Organising Map Analysis",
    align = c("l", "r", "r"),
    escape = TRUE
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down")
  )

cat(som_input_overview_latex)
```

```{r som-grid-evaluation}
# Define Candidate SOM Grid Sizes:
som_grid_options <- tibble(
  Grid = c("8x8", "10x10", "12x12"),
  XDim = c(8, 10, 12),
  YDim = c(8, 10, 12)
)

# Function to Calculate Topographic Error:
calculate_topographic_error <- function(som_model, data_matrix) {
  codes <- som_model$codes[[1]]
  first_bmu <- som_model$unit.classif
  
  data_norm <- rowSums(data_matrix^2)
  code_norm <- rowSums(codes^2)
  
  dist_sq <- outer(data_norm, code_norm, "+") - 2 * data_matrix %*% t(codes)
  dist_sq[cbind(seq_len(nrow(data_matrix)), first_bmu)] <- Inf
  
  second_bmu <- max.col(-dist_sq, ties.method = "first")
  
  unit_distances <- kohonen::unit.distances(som_model$grid)
  
  mean(unit_distances[cbind(first_bmu, second_bmu)] > 1.0001)
}

# Train Candidate SOMs:
som_models <- som_grid_options |>
  mutate(
    Model = pmap(
      list(XDim, YDim),
      \(xdim, ydim) {
        kohonen::som(
          X = som_input,
          grid = kohonen::somgrid(
            xdim = xdim,
            ydim = ydim,
            topo = "hexagonal"
          ),
          rlen = 100,
          alpha = c(0.05, 0.01),
          keep.data = TRUE
        )
      }
    )
  )

# Summarise Candidate Grid Performance:
som_grid_evaluation <- som_models |>
  mutate(
    Map_Units = XDim * YDim,
    Quantisation_Error = map_dbl(Model, ~ mean(.x$distances)),
    Topographic_Error = map_dbl(Model, ~ calculate_topographic_error(.x, som_input))
  ) |>
  select(
    Grid,
    Map_Units,
    Quantisation_Error,
    Topographic_Error
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Quantisation_Error = round(Quantisation_Error, 3),
    Topographic_Error = round(Topographic_Error, 3)
  )

# Output Grid Evaluation Table:
som_grid_evaluation_latex <- som_grid_evaluation |>
  kable(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    caption = "SOM Grid Size Evaluation",
    align = c("l", "r", "r", "r"),
    escape = TRUE
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down")
  )

cat(som_grid_evaluation_latex)
```

```{r som-final-model}
# Select Final SOM Grid:
# The 10x10 grid is used as a practical balance between map detail and interpretability.
som_selected_grid <- "10x10"

# Extract Final SOM Model:
som_model <- som_models |>
  filter(Grid == som_selected_grid) |>
  pull(Model) |>
  pluck(1)

# Save SOM Codebook Vectors:
som_codes <- som_model$codes[[1]]
```

```{r som-training-progress, fig.cap="SOM training progress."}
# Plot SOM Training Progress Manually:

som_training_progress <- as.numeric(som_model$changes)

plot(
  x = seq_along(som_training_progress),
  y = som_training_progress,
  type = "l",
  main = "",
  xlab = "Iteration",
  ylab = "Mean Distance to Closest Unit"
)
```

```{r som-counts-distance, fig.cap="SOM unit counts and neighbour distances."}
# Plot SOM Counts and Neighbour Distances:
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
plot(som_model, type = "counts", main = "SOM Unit Counts")
plot(som_model, type = "dist.neighbours", main = "SOM Neighbour Distances")
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```

```{r som-codebook-cluster-evaluation}
# Evaluate Number of Clusters to Apply to SOM Codebook Vectors:
som_candidate_k <- 2:8

som_code_dist <- dist(som_codes, method = "euclidean")

som_cluster_evaluation <- map_dfr(
  som_candidate_k,
  \(k) {
    set.seed(123)
    km <- kmeans(
      som_codes,
      centers = k,
      nstart = 25
    )
    
    sil <- silhouette(km$cluster, som_code_dist)
    
    tibble(
      K = k,
      Average_Silhouette_Width = mean(sil[, "sil_width"]),
      Total_Within_Variation = km$tot.withinss
    )
  }
) |>
  mutate(
    Average_Silhouette_Width = round(Average_Silhouette_Width, 3),
    Total_Within_Variation = round(Total_Within_Variation, 2)
  )

# Output SOM Cluster Evaluation Table:
som_cluster_evaluation_latex <- som_cluster_evaluation |>
  kable(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    caption = "SOM Codebook Cluster Number Evaluation",
    align = c("r", "r", "r"),
    escape = TRUE
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down")
  )

cat(som_cluster_evaluation_latex)
```

```{r som-cluster-evaluation-plots, fig.cap="SOM codebook cluster-number diagnostics."}
# Plot SOM Codebook Cluster Number Diagnostics:
som_silhouette_plot <- som_cluster_evaluation |>
  ggplot(aes(x = K, y = Average_Silhouette_Width)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = som_candidate_k) +
  labs(
    x = "Number of SOM Clusters (K)",
    y = "Average Silhouette Width"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

som_wcv_plot <- som_cluster_evaluation |>
  ggplot(aes(x = K, y = Total_Within_Variation)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = som_candidate_k) +
  labs(
    x = "Number of SOM Clusters (K)",
    y = "Total Within-Cluster Variation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

som_silhouette_plot
som_wcv_plot
```

```{r som-final-clusters}
# Select Number of SOM Clusters:
# k = 4 is used to align with the main non-hierarchical clustering segmentation and to allow comparison.
som_k <- 4

# Cluster SOM Codebook Vectors:
set.seed(123)
som_code_clusters <- kmeans(
  som_codes,
  centers = som_k,
  nstart = 50
)

# Assign Each Listing to the Cluster of its Best-Matching SOM Unit:
som_cluster <- factor(som_code_clusters$cluster[som_model$unit.classif])

# Add SOM Clusters to the Original Reduced Dataset:
airbnb_som_clustered <- airbnb_reduced |>
  mutate(
    SOM_Cluster = som_cluster
  )

# Create SOM Cluster Size Table:
som_cluster_sizes <- airbnb_som_clustered |>
  count(SOM_Cluster, name = "Listings") |>
  mutate(
    Percentage = percent(Listings / sum(Listings), accuracy = 0.1)
  )

# Output SOM Cluster Sizes Table:
som_cluster_sizes_latex <- som_cluster_sizes |>
  kable(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    caption = "SOM Cluster Sizes",
    align = c("l", "r", "r"),
    escape = TRUE
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down")
  )

cat(som_cluster_sizes_latex)
```

```{r som-cluster-map-clean, fig.cap="SOM map coloured by meta-cluster."}
# Plot Clean SOM Meta-Cluster Map Without Observation Overplotting:

# Convert SOM Codebook Cluster Assignments to a Plain Vector:
som_cluster_vector <- as.integer(som_code_clusters$cluster)

# Set Cluster Colours:
som_cluster_colours <- c(
  "1" = "#F8766D",
  "2" = "#00BA38",
  "3" = "#619CFF",
  "4" = "#C77CFF"
)

# Identify Clusters Actually Present:
present_som_clusters <- sort(unique(som_cluster_vector))

# Allow Space for Legend:
par(mar = c(4, 4, 1, 8), xpd = TRUE)

# Plot Empty SOM Units Coloured by Meta-Cluster:
plot(
  som_model,
  type = "mapping",
  bgcol = som_cluster_colours[as.character(som_cluster_vector)],
  pchs = NA,
  main = ""
)

# Add Cluster Boundaries:
kohonen::add.cluster.boundaries(
  som_model,
  som_cluster_vector
)

# Add Legend Outside the Plot Area:
legend(
  "right",
  inset = c(-0.28, 0),
  legend = paste("SOM Cluster", present_som_clusters),
  fill = som_cluster_colours[as.character(present_som_clusters)],
  border = "black",
  bty = "n",
  cex = 0.8,
  title = "Meta-Cluster"
)

# Reset Plot Margins:
par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1, xpd = FALSE)
```

```{r som-numeric-profile}
# Profile SOM Clusters using Original Numeric Variables:
som_numeric_profile <- airbnb_som_clustered |>
  group_by(SOM_Cluster) |>
  summarise(
    Listings = n(),
    Mean_Price = round(mean(price, na.rm = TRUE), 0),
    Median_Price = round(median(price, na.rm = TRUE), 0),
    Mean_Accommodates = round(mean(accommodates, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Mean_Availability_365 = round(mean(availability_365, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Mean_Number_of_Reviews = round(mean(number_of_reviews, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Mean_Review_Rating = round(mean(review_scores_rating, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Mean_Host_Listings_Count = round(mean(host_listings_count, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Pivot Profile for Report-Friendly Output:
som_numeric_profile_pivot <- som_numeric_profile |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -SOM_Cluster,
    names_to = "Metric",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Metric = str_replace_all(Metric, "_", " ") |>
      str_to_title()
  ) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = SOM_Cluster,
    values_from = Value,
    names_prefix = "SOM Cluster "
  )

# Output SOM Numeric Profile Table:
som_numeric_profile_latex <- som_numeric_profile_pivot |>
  kable(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    caption = "Numeric Profile of SOM Clusters",
    align = "r",
    escape = TRUE
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down")
  )

cat(som_numeric_profile_latex)
```

```{r som-categorical-profile}
# Profile SOM Clusters using Dominant Categorical Values:
som_categorical_profile <- airbnb_som_clustered |>
  group_by(SOM_Cluster) |>
  summarise(
    Most_Common_Neighbourhood = names(sort(table(neighbourhood_cleansed), decreasing = TRUE))[1],
    Most_Common_Property_Type = names(sort(table(property_type_proper_trim), decreasing = TRUE))[1],
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Output SOM Categorical Profile Table:
som_categorical_profile_latex <- som_categorical_profile |>
  kable(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    caption = "Dominant Categorical Characteristics of SOM Clusters",
    align = c("l", "l", "l"),
    escape = TRUE
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down")
  )

cat(som_categorical_profile_latex)
```

```{r som-pca-visualisation, fig.cap="PCA visualisation of the SOM clustering solution."}
# Create PCA Visualisation of SOM Clusters:
som_pca <- prcomp(
  som_input,
  center = FALSE,
  scale. = FALSE
)

som_pca_plot_data <- as_tibble(som_pca$x[, 1:2]) |>
  mutate(
    SOM_Cluster = factor(som_cluster)
  )

som_pca_plot_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, colour = SOM_Cluster)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +
  labs(
    x = "Principal Component 1",
    y = "Principal Component 2",
    colour = "SOM Cluster"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r som-spatial-plot, fig.cap="Spatial distribution of SOM clusters by latitude and longitude."}
# Plot SOM Clusters by Latitude and Longitude:
airbnb_som_clustered |>
  ggplot(aes(x = longitude, y = latitude, colour = SOM_Cluster)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude",
    colour = "SOM Cluster",
    title = "Spatial Distribution of SOM Clusters"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r som-comparison-with-previous}
# Optional Comparison with Previous HC and NHC Results:
# This chunk attempts to compare SOM clusters with prior cluster assignments if those objects exist.

# Compare SOM with HC if the HC sample objects exist:
if (exists("hc_sample_index") && exists("hc_cluster")) {
  som_hc_comparison <- tibble(
    HC_Cluster = factor(hc_cluster),
    SOM_Cluster = som_cluster[hc_sample_index]
  ) |>
    count(HC_Cluster, SOM_Cluster) |>
    group_by(HC_Cluster) |>
    mutate(Percentage = percent(n / sum(n), accuracy = 0.1)) |>
    ungroup()
  
  som_hc_comparison_latex <- som_hc_comparison |>
    kable(
      format = "latex",
      booktabs = TRUE,
      caption = "Comparison of HC and SOM Cluster Assignments",
      align = c("l", "l", "r", "r"),
      escape = TRUE
    ) |>
    kable_styling(
      latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down")
    )
  
  cat(som_hc_comparison_latex)
}

# Compare SOM with NHC/K-means if a full-sample NHC assignment exists:
if (exists("nhc_cluster")) {
  som_nhc_comparison <- tibble(
    NHC_Cluster = factor(nhc_cluster),
    SOM_Cluster = som_cluster
  ) |>
    count(NHC_Cluster, SOM_Cluster) |>
    group_by(NHC_Cluster) |>
    mutate(Percentage = percent(n / sum(n), accuracy = 0.1)) |>
    ungroup()
  
  som_nhc_comparison_latex <- som_nhc_comparison |>
    kable(
      format = "latex",
      booktabs = TRUE,
      caption = "Comparison of NHC and SOM Cluster Assignments",
      align = c("l", "l", "r", "r"),
      escape = TRUE
    ) |>
    kable_styling(
      latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down")
    )
  
  cat(som_nhc_comparison_latex)
}
```

```{r som-save-results}
# Save SOM Results for Use in the Report and Comparative Analysis:
som_results <- list(
  input_data = som_input,
  grid_evaluation = som_grid_evaluation,
  model = som_model,
  codebook_vectors = som_codes,
  codebook_cluster_evaluation = som_cluster_evaluation,
  codebook_clusters = som_code_clusters,
  clusters = som_cluster,
  clustered_data = airbnb_som_clustered,
  cluster_sizes = som_cluster_sizes,
  numeric_profile = som_numeric_profile,
  categorical_profile = som_categorical_profile
)
```

```{r}
#Comparative analysis

# Restrict SOM and NHC assignments to the HC sample
som_sample <- som_cluster[hc_results$sample_index]
nhc_sample <- nhc_results$clusters[hc_results$sample_index]
hc_sample <- hc_results$clusters



# Create percentage table for HC
som_hc_table <- table(
  "SOM Cluster" = som_sample,
  "HC Cluster" = hc_sample
)

som_hc_percent <- round(
  prop.table(som_hc_table, margin = 1) * 100,
  1
)

# Convert to dataframe
som_hc_df <- as.data.frame.matrix(som_hc_percent)

# Create LaTeX table
som_hc_latex <- kable(
  som_hc_df,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  caption = "Percentage Composition of SOM Clusters Across HC Clusters",
  align = "lccc"
)

# Create percentage table for NHC
som_nhc_table <- table(
  "SOM Cluster" = som_sample,
  "NHC Cluster" = nhc_sample
)

som_nhc_percent <- round(
  prop.table(som_nhc_table, margin = 1) * 100,
  1
)

# Convert to dataframe
som_nhc_df <- as.data.frame.matrix(som_nhc_percent)

# Create LaTeX table
som_nhc_latex <- kable(
  som_nhc_df,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  caption = "Percentage Composition of SOM Clusters Across NHC Clusters",
  align = "lccc"
)


#Silhouette comparison
silhouette_comparison <- tibble(
  Method = c("Hierarchical Clustering",
             "Non-Hierarchical Clustering",
             "Self-Organising Map"),
  `Average silhouette width` = c(
    mean(hc_results$silhouette[, "sil_width"]),
    nhc_results$silhouette_width$silhouette[
      nhc_results$silhouette_width$k == nhc_results$best_k
    ],
    silhouette_scores[som_k - min(som_candidate_k) + 1]
  )
)

silhouette_comparison |>
  kable(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    digits = 3,
    caption = "Comparison of Average Silhouette Widths Across Clustering Methods",
    align = "lcc"
  )
```
